projects.name: Homelab Foundation
projects.description: Set up TrueNAS SCALE on a Dell Optiplex 3070 SFF with a dual-pool storage layout — a 3TB HDD pool for media and a separate SSD pool for app configs. All services run as Docker containers managed through Dockge, exposed to the internet securely via Cloudflare Zero Trust tunnels with no open ports required.

projects.name: Media Ecosystem
projects.description: Built a complete media stack: Jellyfin handles streaming with Intel QuickSync hardware transcoding, while Sonarr, Radarr, Prowlarr, and qBittorrent work together to automatically find and download content. All download traffic is routed through NordVPN via Gluetun for privacy.

projects.name: Photo & Game Libraries
projects.description: Immich provides fully self-hosted photo and video backup with direct integration to the NAS storage. Romm manages a retro game library of over 2,300 titles across 19 platforms, complete with cover art and metadata pulled automatically from IGDB.

projects.name: Storage Expansion
projects.description: Planning to add an 8TB drive and migrate the current media pool to expand capacity. The goal is to keep the existing folder structure intact so nothing breaks — Jellyfin, Sonarr, and Radarr all rely on consistent paths for hardlinking.

projects.name: Hardware Upgrade
projects.description: The Optiplex 3070 has been a great starting point, but upgrading to a system with more drive bays, PCIe slots, and ECC RAM support is the next step. This will open the door to larger storage pools and more demanding workloads down the road.

automation.name: Automated Media Downloads
automation.description: Sonarr and Radarr monitor for new releases and automatically trigger downloads through qBittorrent the moment something becomes available. Once downloaded, files are moved, renamed, and organized — no manual steps required.

automation.name: Quality Profile Sync
automation.description: Recyclarr runs on a schedule to keep Radarr and Sonarr in sync with TRaSH Guide quality profiles. This ensures release scoring, preferred formats, and quality cutoffs stay tuned without needing to dig through settings manually.

automation.name: Cloudflare Tunnel Management
automation.description: A Cloudflared container maintains persistent tunnels for all six public subdomains. When a new service needs to be exposed, a new tunnel entry is added in the Cloudflare dashboard — no firewall changes, no router config, no downtime.

automation.name: Uptime Monitoring
automation.description: Uptime Kuma checks every service at regular intervals and pushes alerts when something goes down. A public status page at status.gonzotech.us shows the live state of the whole stack — useful for sharing uptime with anyone who uses the services.
